reviews.review_description:
I have had no issues with this application what so ever, once it is installed I just connect using Remote Desktop through the headset, makes life easy and I can use the headset browser to watch movies or tv while using my PC. 

Only downside with this app would be if you have dedicated router or wired connection you cannot use it, its like it goes to a server and back to your headset. 

Another downside is that it hasn't really had any updates and if they could do curved monitors with 3 compact screens open it would be great because with 3 flat monitors you tend to miss some information at the ends of the monitors off to the side. If the monitors are set to portrait then it looks find not curved.

I did like that it feels like part of quest and windows eco-system once setup so that is a plus.

reviews.review_title: The last update broke the app
reviews.review_description:
Since the last update this week (2026.03.20) it is not possible to connect to a computer using this app. When it should display the instructions and read the qr code from the pc, it doesn't display anything at all.. 
It wasn't very useful before but it is unusable now...

Tried to pair my Quest 3 with my Windows 11 PC. The Windows Mixed Reality Link app gives me a QR to scan, but the Quest app shows no window when I click "I have the app". I will change my review if it is fixed and starts working.

reviews.review_title: Great but stoped to work correctly
reviews.review_description: I would give it 5 Stars, it's worked great, but suddenly the upper menu not appearing anymore and I can't change the display option. Also the hands recognition still works even through I disabled it initially from the menu. So the application is great but the function I liked not working anymore.
The app worked fine for me but the amount of issues that were mentioned in the reviews maybe from Microsoft fixing the issues but for now it worked fine for me.

I was under the impression that this app would let me use my computer programs inside VR just like I would on a normal screen, not just mirror the display. Right now, it seems like all it’s doing is mirroring and I can’t really interact the way I expected. Could you let me know if I’m using it wrong or if there’s another way to do it?

reviews.review_title: Good RDP App in VR from Microsoft
reviews.review_description:
Implementation of Microsoft's remote desktop software in VR for free. Working on windows machines is fast, stable and because of RDP there is no additional software needed on PC.
The app is identical to the Android version of MS RDP app. 
Perfect for working or controlling the PC. For remote gaming, I would prefer Virtual Desktop.
The is worst pcvr experience ever, and no matter what I do it doesn't seem to function preperly; at all. Fix this thing please.

First off, wasn't this supposed to be a fairly seamless experience? I remember seeing that once set up, you look at your computer and you can instantly mirror your screen in VR. I'm going through hoops of fire to get this connected to my new computer and all I get is a spinning loading screen. Classic meta
The latest version of this app doesn't even give me the option to connect to a local pc so it's unusable

Virtually (Almost) Perfect... 

✔️ Text is legible in any mode: Compact, Standard, Wide, Ultra Wide.

✔️ Set up is easy: scan a QR code on your PC, follow prompts.

✔️ Focus Mode: Dims the background

✔️ Resizeable windows; change the aspect ratio 


➖️ Needs to be truly 'Remote' that means no need to be on the same Wifi network as my PC.

➖️ Needs to include the use of Touch Controllers to interact with the Virtual Monitors. 

➖️ Needs to include the use of Hand Tracking to interact with the Virtual Monitors, both with the hand laser and by actually touching the screen. 

➖️ Needs to permit the use of Bluetooth keyboard and mouse connected to the headset itself to actually be truly 'Remote'.


☆☆☆ 
My recommendation is to offer a native Virtual Phone app that displays your phone screen in VR. 
Maybe through the use of Link to Windows

Meta for business account required?? realy?

Windows app on android, ios, mac or pc.. Fine. Meta? sign up for and pay for a meta for business account or no go. This is not ms making this play, its 100% meta.

update: meta for business no longer required!

Works great for my Win365 sub and for my AVD host.

Thanks for listening to community feedback Meta/MS!!

include an option for using a ultrawide screen resolution, instead of only an option for three 1080p90 virtual monitors, for example allow the Quest virtual screen to be 21:9 or 32:9 aspect ratios. ultrawide  monitor resolution. also needs option to be able to forward the controls of a bluetooth mouse and keyboard connected to the Quest headset

UPDATE: I am not sure what the requirements are for ultrawide mode? I have seen screenshots of where it is, but for my Win 11 computer it only allows adding 2 extra windows
I love it!
Not sure what the point of this app is, definitely doesn't work with meta quest 3 and Windows 11 via Gigabit wireless network and with 2gbps internet.
It’s way better to use virtual desktop despite having to pay for it.
Super.
All this app offers is the opportunity to stare at a QR code on your laptop while waiting in vain for anything to happen.
Scan qr code won't work no matter what I do
it is very hard to get past the qr code. it takes about 20 times to try and get the qr code to finally scan

reviews.review_title: Why do I need a meta business account?
reviews.review_description: This looks great, but I can’t even try it. I have an azure virtual desktop access available on my PC and iPad. It works really well and has no additional account requirements. I think requiring a meta business account ill impact VR adoption on quest more than the revenue meta business accounts would generate . Please just give me the ability to log into my work AVD.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your feedback!  I'm happy to report that the latest version of the Windows App allows you to connect to your Azure Virtual Desktop without a Horizon Managed Services (Quest for Business) account.

reviews.review_description: I was surprised to see how good this app is. it's working very well for a free app. It's worth using

There goes local storage of anything. There'll be no hardware needed. PC will be done by cloud just like this.  Sounds great unless you like to keep your stuff close like I do. I just like to  know it's there and nobody else has it.   There goes the rest of your privacy as well. At war for your privacy and Americans never even fired off a shot. Please, please, please take this away from Microsoft.

I was/am intending to use this app to access my works Windows 365 cloudpc directly, but apparently, for some reason, a Meta for Business account is required to use the cloudpc option, which is super-dumb if you're running zero trust and 2-factor authentification to create a BYOD-environment. Please remove that requirement as it messes with companies that have a BYOD-setup as well as ordinary, non-business related people who wants to use Windows 365 cloudpc.

I know Meta REAAALLY wants us to use their business suite tools, but stop making it a requirement as it will hamper adoption and create a chicken and egg problem when it comes to using VR for work.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your feedback!  I'm happy to report that the latest version of the Windows App allows you to connect to your Windows 365 Cloud PC without a Horizon Managed Services (Quest for Business) account.

reviews.review_title: Ok, but still has a long way to go
reviews.review_description:
It connects easily, but disconnects almost as easily. It does not allow you to see the keyboard in immersive or theater mode. It DOES darken the computersl screen, which along with good earbuds, allows for some privacy, but you can't  adjust the big screen very well, to change size or distance. 
My preference  would be a window for the keyboard (similar to another good program) in immersive or allow the Bluetooth  keyboard to show and be useable without having to actually see it.
The app work only one time and then it don’t want to work again
Cant even connect after it turned into windows app 👎
They won't send me the email to download it, and all of the websites I search for and instructions on the internet are bs

reviews.review_title: App is slow, and only work through Wi-Fi
reviews.review_description: I wish this app could work not only with Wi-Fi but also with the USB-C cable, which may reduce lagging and increase performance. The only good thing I like about this app (and this  is the only reason I prefer using this app), is when you connect your VR headset to the computer through this app, my laptop monitor goes black, providing with a layer of privacy to those with want to be noisy about what you are doing on your computer when you can not see them because the VR headset

reviews.review_title: Great concept, but does need some work..
reviews.review_description:
Pros: can look at your laptop to connect easily; love the ability to have one larger curved screen or up to three; feels immersive and helps to keep me focused; can easily and privately work anywhere; you can use peripherals connected to your laptop (don’t need to connect them to the VR headset); resolution for general work is clear; FREE.


Cons: sometimes laggy and it would be nice to potentially have a network monitor so we could see if there is an issue with the connection; when you remove the headset and put it back on, it should auto-launch and remember your screen configuration because that is a hassle to setup every time; sometimes video coming through was choppy be affected sound too.

I think it is moving in the right direction, but definitely have some opportunities for improvement. ￼

This app works great, I use it way more then virtual desktop when controlling my PC from another room

Virtual desktop has a big delay using my pc's keyboard and mouse while the windows 11 app works fine...

The windows 11 app allows for 3 screens at 1080p and it looks great and performs really good connecting to a pc using a prismXR puppies S1(dedicated router)

The only thing I don't like about this app is once you move your screens into position and get the perfect size all is good until you either take off the headset or you disconnect, all the screens reset and it's a pain to move them over and over again, it would be nice if the app could remember the screen sizes and positions

I thought I could connect my keyboard/mouse with my quest 3 and use it as input for my computer. It looks like, it is just a mirror app where you have to be on your computers keyboard and mouse 

I uninstalled it quickly because I am using Virtual Desktop which is awesome. I am also enable to use my quest 3 controllers as mouse or as gamepad in game.

2 major problems are need to be fixed

1. low bitrate (when it compares to other methods) which cause color fringing and blurry mosaic randomly.  
2. totally messing PC's DPI setting after quit. some of apps staying as original, but others being changed window size too much especially with 150~200% dpi setting with 4k monitor.

I think this is really good not sure why the reviews are so poor. It’s a preview and if it’s actually being developed it’s already a winner. Some of the reviews are old so would be useful to see a dev history. Rather than go into a working environment it’s simply remote screens you can use alongside everything else and position anywhere. I can’t get decent mic passthrough on workrooms, vspatial or immersed which makes them unusable if you use an IP phone for work. This app gives me three very clear pc virtual displays in my headset environment. I run teams and 3CX in the headset browser next to them which solves the mic issue, and the headset comes with nice environment as standard and deals with keyboard passthrough.
Developers, if you can get the mouse passthrough working so I can have a Bluetooth mouse connected to the headset like VSpatial that works in the headset and seamlessly passes throughwould be perfect.
I use my Quest over VPN to access my Windows 11 PC, but I need to use an app on my phone to enable passthrough peripherals. Why doesn't the win app on the quest3 handle this directly??

reviews.review_title: I think I am using a different app....
reviews.review_description:
.....brcause this one is getting review bombed for no reason. It's free and it's just awesome. Easy to setup, in matter of minutes you will have your pc's monitor multiplied x 3 and HUGE. Watching movies from my pc, in passthrough, is simply priceless. Playing videogame on a massive curved momitor is the bomb.

So yeah. I am sorry it has an average of 2,9 stars, it says a lot about the flawed Meta's review system, where a game like "I am cat" is one of hte highest ranked on Horizon. Nothing against it, but I find it hard to even call it a game.

As I'm using 6 monitors on my workstation (Yes, I'm in crypto, how could you tell?), it only let's you use the first 3 monitors for some reason (yes, 3 at a time is advertised, but you can't choose which one). It doesn't let you use your quest controllers or hand gestures as mouse replacement which would be okay if it weren't for Windows forgetting the display layout which is a real pain. Unusable for me and always a lot of work rearranging and setting orientation to correct when I want to use my actual monitors (which is 99.9% of the time) again. I didn't watch a video or something but for browsing it works.

I really want to test this as a POC, with AVD and my personal headset. Unfortunately it needs to be connected to a Meta Business account to do this. I can't seem to do this on a personal headset without factory resetting. 

Please remove he Meta Business requirement for AVD. 

If this works it could be a great replacement for our 10Zig thin clients.

The app works well enough as a remote desktop solution, very easy to configure and connect, but is lacking options like curved screens, an option to save the monitors I want and the positions, and more resolution options. Also there should be a setting to mute the VR environment while connected to the computer. 

I like the app, but cannot use it for full time productivity because of those lack of features.

Gives a max 1080p screen projection (up to 3x screens) or one large curved screen.

Q3S would not install the app until I used the store link (auto link just sat with a spinning circle).

Q3S controllers will only interact with the app, not the pc.

The pc backlit keyboard and mouse is fully visible in AR and settled on one large curved screen.
Tried a few games using keyboard and mouse with the large projected screen floating in front of me, fallout 76 whilst only 1080p felt very immersive as the screen enveloped you whilst playing.

GPU demand dropped considerably compared to steam link whilst using this method on a 12gb 3080ti max gaming unit.
Steam link still does it better complete with pc interaction, up your game guys.
Add multiple screens or connections at the same time then it will be great else, its just another wannabe app

It’s not possible to connect to a corporate account, not even for evaluation purpose.

Given it’s an early access version, that doesn’t make much sense :/

At first it connected straight away, the next time it wouldn't connect, then it said the graphic card needed updating, then it connected, when to use again and said again the graphic card needs updating. Spare yourself hours of messing about just use the pc
The clarity is not good enough, and you cannot adjust the resolution and frame rate. The audio is very poor, especially when watching movies. I suggest you learn from virtual desktop.
Doesn't work 😐

Easy to set up and works perfectly from my laptop. Once paired just facing towards my screen from a few feet starts the connection. Only thing I needed to do was disable hardware exceleration on Google  to stream some online movie content otherwise it wouldn't cast. Other than that I cant fault it.

reviews.review_title: App crashing when trying to use corp acc
reviews.review_description: Trying to set up w365, but it crashes at organization logon page.  Also its not clear on here that the w365 option only works with Quest for business.  What is the purpose of that?  We're already paying for the w365 instances and its just a remote desktop app.  Immersed lets you do all this with w365 for free.  Why should anyone have to have additional business costs for quest.

reviews.review_title: Amazing concept that just doesn't work
reviews.review_description:
I got this working just one time, and it was really great to extend my Surface Laptop 7 to three screens. The resolution of each virtual screen is lower, but 1080p is in no way unusable. One little touch I really like is that the Quest recognizes my laptop and prompts me to connect to it!

Sadly, after my initial success I kept running into errors with Remote Desktop. The app continuously told me to restart it on my computer. I can connect, but typing anything causes the connection to hang. Only closing the Windows App on the Quest gives me control again.

I very much want this to work. Is there some sort of Insider program I can join for this experience to help improve it? I'll keep up with this app in hopes it improves in time.

Windows 11.
V72.
Option activated in Q3.
Same network wifi6
The instant I scan de QR code a message displaying "failed to pair, cancel and restart the app" is displayed on quest 3.
Why must we waste our time troubleshooting every app we download? Done the things. got the links. download the files. run the apps. It doesn't connect to the PC. says it's not available.

reviews.review_title: Works but did take a little fiddling
reviews.review_description: This app works as intended but the interface is a tad unintuitive. Why was the fist screen of the app a large blue button that envoked the meta Remote Desktop? I had to close that pop up window and proceed to setup my credentials of the pc. After enabling Remote Desktop on windows a disabling the authentication port I was able to get a successful connection. The controllers work and you can even use your hands for gestures and touchscreen esc input. It seems to be working pretty good and I even get to see my room in pass through mode. Can I not share my camera to the remote pc? Every time I tried to enable the permission it was not showing under settings or gave me a pop up so that is weird. Works great so far but I also install fresh windows 11 just to try this feature out.

reviews.review_title: This is exactly what I expected it to be
reviews.review_description:
Remote Desktop alone was a flop due to only being able to use one screen. However, this beauty is exactly as advertised!

I tested it in various setups mainly doing finances (accounting), studying, and hacking a few machines, as stated in the header, it works quite well.

The ability to disable the physical screens and use 3 screens through the Quest is a big plus for privacy in public environments or against shoulder surfers.

Setting up isn’t that hard either. Due to my personal setup, which is highly focused on security, i got it working through this app on the Quest and the following two on a Windows 11 client;
- Remote Desktop for Windows (through the Meta/Oculus website)
- Mixed Reality Link (Windows Store)

Having both Remote Desktop for Windows and Mixed Reality Link running, while starting Windows App (preview) it gave me instructions and shortly after, everything was up and running.

To finish; some advice for those complaining that Windows 10 isn’t available: there is absolutely no reason to still use 10 unless your hardware doesn’t support it. There is no use for legacy systems. Protect yourself and upgrade!

reviews.review_title: I’m very excited about this integration
reviews.review_description: I spend hours sitting at my desk every day for work, and I can already see myself using this app to work from my couch, bed, and recliner. I’m also excited to see Microsoft integrating Windows with Quest, and I hope they continue developing and improving the experience. I tested the app and it’s working great, very stable over a regular wifi signal. I’m getting a new wireless keyboard and mouse to bring my pc with me to different corners of my house. Great job.
but first i went through the the easy set  up disappeared by next day was brilliant though no i manually filled id and works but cant use the mouse pc
Still can't figure out what this does over remote desktop. It still seems to require you to be at your pc using a mouse connected to it.

most users are still on windows 10 because of different reasons (game performance, update chaos, taskbar [cannot be docked to the sides nativly, right?], etc) and this app is not compatible with windows 10.

I’m loving the experience so far.
Streaming latency is really low and my 3 virtual screens all stream at 90hz according to Windows and it certainly looks like 90hz to my eye.

Ease of use is excellent too, just a really good experience apart from 2 things…

1) Please stop it disconnecting and killing the connection if the Quest3 is taken off the head for a split second.

2) Have the option to remember the virtual window setup / positions etc.
Seamless way to get a tri-monitor setup. Just may lag if your PCs GPU is obviously under heavy load.
It worked well but it was really laggy

I like how i’m able to use this without direct internet connection with my pc, being able to connect without being home. But, the issue i’m having with this app is that, there is no audio. I think if they were to add audio coming from the quest 3, this app wouldn’t be so bad.
